title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Design Defense
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an MCP security test suite and operating policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

steps.title: Server Trust · changed case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments.
